# Copilot instructions for LifeOS (Obsidian vault)

Purpose
- Help future Copilot sessions understand build/test workflows, architecture, and repository conventions so suggestions and edits are accurate and safe.

Primary locations
- Vault root (Obsidian): LifeOS/
- Automation & scripts: LifeOS/Scripts/
- DB schema: LifeOS/Config/database/schema.sql and phase4_pgvector.sql
- Core docs: LifeOS/README.md and LifeOS/Config/*.md

Build / test / lint (what exists)
- Install Python deps: pip install -r LifeOS\Scripts\requirements.txt
- Run daily automation (bundled tasks): python LifeOS\Scripts\run_daily_automation.py
- Sync vault (validate only): python LifeOS\Scripts\sync_vault.py --validate-only
- Index embeddings / migrate: python LifeOS\Scripts\index_embeddings.py --migrate
- Semantic search: python LifeOS\Scripts\semantic_search.py "query" [--context]
- Backup: python LifeOS\Scripts\backup_lifeos.py [--skip-db]
- Tests: pytest LifeOS\Scripts\tests

How to run a single test
- By file: pytest LifeOS\Scripts\tests\test_file.py
- By class or method: pytest LifeOS\Scripts\tests\test_file.py::TestClass::test_method
- Quick selection: pytest -k "test_name_substring"

High-level architecture (concise)
- Interface: Obsidian vault is the primary UI; markdown files are source-of-truth.
- Knowledge: Notes + YAML frontmatter provide metadata; Dataview/Tasks/Tracker power dashboards.
- Structured layer: PostgreSQL stores normalized records (tasks, habits, finance, analytics, embeddings metadata) but NOT raw note bodies.
- Automation: Python scripts in LifeOS/Scripts parse notes, sync structured records, and index embeddings.
- AI/RAG: Local chunking -> local embeddings (sentence-transformers or hash fallback) -> metadata+pgvector (Phase 4) -> compressed context -> local LLM (Ollama) or remote LLM.

Key conventions and patterns (important for edits)
- Data ownership: "Markdown is truth" — never persist raw note bodies to the DB. Only metadata, summaries, chunk ranges, and embeddings are stored.
- Minimal frontmatter: keep type, status, created, updated, tags. New templates should extend this schema.
- Naming: daily notes YYYY-MM-DD.md; weekly/monthly as in Config/Vault Structure.md; projects/goals templates use explicit prefixes.
- Embeddings & token strategy: store short summaries and chunk metadata in DB; read raw chunk text from disk only when needed and compress context before LLM calls.
- Scripts are environment-driven: expect .env in LifeOS/Scripts and provider toggles via LIFEOS_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER (e.g., hash).
- Plugin expectations: Dataview, Tasks, Templater, QuickAdd, Tracker — dashboards are Dataview-first and should remain portable (avoid JS where possible).
- Migration path: Phase 3 uses text-backed embedding storage; Phase 4 adds pgvector and a schema migration (phase4_pgvector.sql).

When editing or suggesting code
- Preserve the "no raw-body in DB" rule in any DB or API changes.
- Prefer small, surgical edits localized to LifeOS/Scripts for automation changes.
- For new features, point to where Python scripts, DB schema, and templates must change together.
- When adding retrieval logic, add metadata columns (line range, note path, summary) and avoid saving whole note content.

Useful quick references for Copilot
- Run local scripts from vault root: python LifeOS\Scripts\<script>.py
- Tests: pytest LifeOS\Scripts\tests
- DB schema: LifeOS\Config\database\schema.sql
- Embedding migration: LifeOS\Config\database\phase4_pgvector.sql

AI/assistant-specific guidance
- Avoid hallucinating production-grade secrets (DB passwords, API keys). Use environment variable placeholders.
- If proposing an LLM change, prefer local-first (Ollama) or clearly label cloud-only steps.
- No assistant config files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, etc.) were found; integrate changes into Config/ if needed.

Where to look next
- LifeOS/Config/ for setup, Plugin Setup.md, Vault Structure.md, and Phase 4 Semantic Retrieval.md
- LifeOS/Scripts/ for implementation and tests

Contact
- When in doubt about acceptable schema changes, open an issue in the repo with schema migration proposals and a rollback plan.

---
Autogenerated on behalf of repository owner. Please review and expand with any CI commands, linters, or deployment steps if added later.

MCP server configuration
- A recommended MCP server manifest was added: .github/mcp_servers.yml
- It documents a docker-compose-style Postgres 15+ local service and optional Ollama LLM notes.
- Use the docker_compose_service snippet to spin up Postgres for local testing; set DATABASE_URL in LifeOS/Scripts/.env and never commit real credentials.

If you want, Copilot sessions can use the MCP server definitions to suggest infrastructure edits, schema migrations, or CI service setups. Ensure secrets are stored in environment variables or CI secrets (do not store passwords in repo files).
